ops.title: How to release @cc-interact/react and cc-present together, and the bun-link trap while the npm version is unpublished
ops.body:
Releasing the cc-interact → cc-present pair (learned shipping rounds, 2026-07-08):

- **cc-interact npm release**: bump `web/package.json`, entry under CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` (the changelog's `## [x.y.z]` sections track the Go core, not npm — npm 0.3.0 got no section), one feature commit, tag `v<npm version>` → `release.yml` publishes `@cc-interact/react` via CI (skips already-published). Publish is CI-only ([[publish-via-ci-only]]).
- **cc-present release**: `chore(release): X` commit touching `CHANGELOG.md` (real `## [X] - date` section) + `plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` version, then tag `vX`. prek git hooks run golangci-lint on commit.
- **bun-link trap**: while the new @cc-interact/react is unpublished, cc-present builds need `bun link` — but cc-present's `task build` runs `bun install`, which silently re-resolves from the registry and REPLACES the link symlink. Re-apply `bun link @cc-interact/react` after every `bun install`, or run `bunx vite build` + `go build` directly. Before committing `bun.lock`, `rm -rf node_modules/@cc-interact && bun install` and check the lock entry has a registry sha512, and node_modules holds a real directory, not a symlink.
- **Sequencing**: land + tag cc-interact first, wait for npm (`npm view @cc-interact/react@X version`), then bump cc-present's `^X` + lockfile + rebuild the `internal/web/dist` embed in the same commit as the UI that needs it.
- cc-pool working copies sit on detached HEADs that go stale; `git checkout --detach origin/main` carries a dirty tree over cleanly when upstream didn't touch your files ([[parallel-sessions-own-the-working-copy]]).
